candidates.stmt: Richard has lived in Old Basing for over 10 years and works locally as a dentist. His children attend a local school where he is a parent governor. He enjoys outdoor pursuits and is passionate about preserving and protecting the environment.

I‘m proud to have served the community of Bramley Parish Council for ten years where I live. As Basingstoke expands, I am conscious of how difficult it gets to protect the integrity of our villages and small communities.  It matters to have experience in dealing with such major issues. I am a retired business man and now have the time and desire to serve the wider Bramley Ward as a Borough Councillor. Political agendas get in the way of representing people openly and transparently. The Independents are not a lone voice as they represent the many voices of the community.

Adam lives in Bramley ward with his wife and son and works nearby as an electronic engineer. Outside of work, he served five years as a school governor, and has undertaken a number of other volunteering roles. He is a keen football fan and amateur cricketer.

If elected, Adam would seek to be a strong voice for the community in council matters and meet regularly with residents to discuss issues that they are facing. His priorities include protecting the natural environment, supporting residents through the Tory cost-of-living crisis, and making the local area an attractive place for all to live and work.

My name is Ashlynn and I live and work in Brookvale and Kings Furlong. I grew up near Alton and have been a visitor to Basingstoke since childhood and have lived and worked here for nearly 3 years. I work for the NHS in the drug and alcohol service supporting the people of Basingstoke and further afield in accessing recovery.
I grew up through financial crashes and failing local services that let more and more of us fall through the cracks each year.

I feel that the old two party system has been letting us down as long as I've been alive and am keen to represent the Green party in our bold vision for a politics of hope that can steer our area and nation back on track. This way we can look after eachother in order to live comfortable lives.

An experienced local councillor, Michael is standing to represent the ward he calls home.

First elected in 2021 and re-elected with an increased majority, Michael has a strong track record of delivering for residents and building local support. Now standing in the area where he lives, he brings both experience and a direct personal stake in the community.

Since joining the Green Party, he has played a key role in growing Green representation and showing that a different kind of politics is possible. Alongside his council work, he is a Network Engineer, a volunteer magistrate, and is studying for a Masters in Environmental Science.

He is focused on protecting the environment, strengthening communities, and ensuring the economy works for everyone, not just a few.

Resident and neighbour in Kempshott and Buckskin.
Bringing people together has always been my passion. I have organised community events, was a Girlguiding member for over 30yrs, previously a trustee of BACP and currently volunteer as a driver for Neighbour Care.
With my background as an accountant and commercial leader, I am committed to working for the changes the residents of this ward need.

I grew up here in Kempshott & Buckskin, my family is rooted here, and I plan to raise my own family here. But I’m tired of seeing the same problems talked about year after year without real solutions.
From pedestrian safety on Pack Lane to the lack of clear communication on regeneration in Buckskin, our community deserves better than unresolved issues.

I have the energy, the time, and the determination to commit to this for the long haul. I’m here to work, and I’m ready to put everything I have into making it happen.

Kempshott & Buckskin – Catherine Hopkins
Catherine is a retired solicitor and former nurse and midwife who worked in the NHS, saying she saw “first hand” the strain of years of cuts. After three decades living and working locally and raising four children here, she wants better GP services and a “local voice in planning decisions” so new housing comes with the right infrastructure.
(source - Basingstoke Gazette online)

I am delighted to be standing as the Liberal Democrat candidate for the Kempshott and Buckskin ward in the Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council elections on Thursday 7th May. I hope to build on the successes of the current Liberal Democrat / Independent administration, which include protecting Down Grange from golf course development and prioritising Old Down as a Local Nature Reserve. 
I have lived in Basingstoke for nearly 40 years. Shortly after moving here, i helped to found the Basingstoke Heritage Society and became its first Chairman - I am now the Life President. My main career was as an architect, but I am also a musician.

I am asking for your support to serve our entire community as an independent councillor, free to speak up for you. My commitment is that Oakley and our villages will always come first.
I believe communities know their own needs best. What they often lack isn’t ideas. It’s genuine support, access to the right people, and someone to connect them and make things happen. I believe in politics that delivers: practical, local, and focused on what actually helps. Independence isn’t about going it alone. It’s about putting local people first.
That’s why being an independent matters. I believe our communities deserve a strong local voice – someone who puts people before politics. That’s why it’s so important to have someone from our community speaking up for us.

My focus for Oakley
•
Speak up and support plans for the green spaces between Oakley and any planned development, protecting our village’s character and separation.
•
I will continue to work with the Parish Council on improving our open spaces and play areas.
•
Press for practical improvements to road safety, tackling excesssive speed on our local roads. Including safer crossings and better junction layouts.
•
Work to ensure Oakley residents and the Parish Council are involved early in decisions that affect the village.
•
Push for infrastructure, investment in doctors and schools, to be secured early and delivered alongside development.
•
Encourage shared facilities in new developments to be publicly or community managed for the benefit of the wider village. I don’t support management companies.

My local experience
•
Parish Councillor, Oakley and Deane Parish Council – former Chair, now focused on hands-on community impact.
•
Founder, Our Oakley – creating a shared place for community information and events in Oakley.
•
Trustee & Committee Member, Oakley Community Association – helping run village events and support community work.
•
Area Coordinator, Oakley Neighbourhood Watch – supporting coordinators and residen

I’m Dr Marc Connor and I am standing alongside Alex Rowley asking for your vote in the May 7th elections. Both of your Conservative borough councillors have stood down. That means you will have two votes in the borough council elections this year.
Cllr Julian Jones, was your first Independent Councillor elected back in 2024. Alex Rowley and I are local people committed to supporting our local communities. Together we are a team of independents.
I live in Bradley, in our ward, and I’m the Chair of the Parish Meeting and I’ve served as an Independent councillor. Alex lives in Oakley, he’s a Parish Councillor and past Chair of Oakley and Deane Parish Council. Together we live in the ward we want to represent.
We are not tied to a national political party telling us how to vote or what to think. We are focused on our communities first and always. These elections are not about national politics, nor are they about the same old political parties that have failed us. We want to be councillors for the place we live and communities we care about.
We are asking you to use your two votes for the both of us, for your independent team. This is about a positive vote for our community.
I’m a public health professional and I am committed to public service. I have worked in health & justice research for over 20 years, research which has contributed towards improving health and reducing crime at the local, regional and national levels.
If you have any questions please do not hesitate to get in touch with us. We are going to knock on as many doors as we can over the next few weeks, we’ve been working all over the ward. From Ellisfield to Bradley, from Dummer to North Waltham. You will have had leaflets from us all year round.
Help us change the way we do politics in Oakley, let’s get off the party political merry-go-round and focus on our local issues.

An Oakley and Deane Parish Councillor, administrator for Basingstoke Business and Enterprise forum. Sherrie is completing a PHD in International Business. A published author and counsellor, she says she is “dedicated to empowering residents, supporting vulnerable people and most highly on her agenda is protecting our villages from creeping urbanisation, our countryside ecology needs protection from over development.”

Karl is a long-standing local resident with strong roots in the area. Having lived in Rooksdown, Monk Sherborne and now Marnel Park, he knows the community and its challenges first-hand.

After a career in customer service and the NHS, Karl is focused on delivering practical results for local people — from protecting our villages from overdevelopment to improving local facilities and tackling everyday issues.

Karl is standing to be a strong local voice who will work hard, listen to residents, and get things done.

From soaring rents and mortgage costs to painful waits for NHS treatment, the fallout from the Conservatives’ broken promises has been felt by us all.

It’s time for change.

Whilst our current MP endorsed Liz Truss’s disastrous economic policies, Labour has a costed and optimistic vision for the future. I promise to deliver on the things that matter to you, putting people over politics and representing our towns and villages with pride.

Bradley Phillips

In a Nutshell … I am a dedicated public servant with experience working in education and the voluntary sector across Hampshire. Like many, I’ve relied on our public services throughout my life – particularly the NHS, after I was diagnosed with arthritis aged 3. I know how important these services are for so many, and improving them is my biggest priority.

My Story I’m 25 years old and was born and raised in Basingstoke.

I have overcome numerous challenges which have shaped my values and passions today. At the age of 3 I was diagnosed with arthritis, meaning that I have relied on the NHS from an early age.

My father worked as a train driver and then lorry driver. My mother is disabled and raised us as a single mum. My family has faced financial hardship and, at times, has had to rely on food banks.

We fell between the cracks in the social security system for ordinary families. Caring for my mum – alongside my sister – has further deepened my understanding of our society’s support structures and the desperate need for a real solution to our health and social care crisis.

As a proud gay man I am a strong advocate for the importance of equality, inclusion and representation at all levels of society.

My Future Plan If elected, I promise to listen to your concerns, represent your interests, and earn your trust.

Labour’s plan for growth will deliver the stability our local businesses and public services need to thrive.

I will strive for investment in our local economy and hold government to account, locally and n

Tony Jones has served as councillor for Buckskin ward for 24 of the last 28 years. He is looking forward to using his long standing ties with South Ham and helping residents with their issues. Tony is no stranger to fighting for his ward and was instrumental in  securing  local flood defences.

I have lived in Tadley since moving here in 1969 with a
young family. I joined Tadley Town Council in 1983
wanting to improve facilities for my young family and
fellow residents.I have been re-elected to the Town
Council ever since and for two periods was also a
Borough councillor for Tadley.
As a Town Councillor I have served as Chairman on
several occasions, each time making a difference to the
facilities and improving the environment. I was a
founder member of Tadley Citizens Advice and still
serve as a trustee. I am Chair of Governors at the
Bishopswood schools and also Chair of Barlow’s Park
community football ground which I helped to deliver
for the community in 2008. As you can see I believe in a
hands on role feeling that you get out what you put in.
I have had a varied career, working for the Automobile
Association, a Housing Association and finally, for 18
years for the Environment Agency where I led on
community engagement and media communications
until I retired last year.
My long time interest in environmental issues
encouraged me to become an unpaid Director of
Calleva Community Energy installing solar on roofs
locally. Having introduced Tadley Spring Clean week I
have continued to promote green issues now through
engagement with the Tadley Environment Group.

In this area it's the Lib Dems who are seen working hard for residents all year round.

I'm proud to have served as your Borough Councillor. I've achieved alot in four years and I have much more to do.

I hope I have gained your trust and you will re-elect me in May, so I can continue my local community work and also maintain the momentum we have in running a high performing Borough Council.

Kerry

I’ve lived in Whitchurch all of my life, my parents were both born in Overton so I have extended family on both sides of the hill! I’m a mum of three girls who attend Whitchurch Primary and Testbourne schools and work as a Director at a major finance company based in Basingstoke. I have always felt incredibly lucky to be part of a strong community. Whilst Whitchurch and Overton have grown over the years, that sense of belonging remains. I stand as an independent because people work better together without the agenda of party politics getting in the way of a good conversation.

My husband, youngest daughter and I moved to this area 29 years ago. I soon discovered how lovely this part of Hampshire is as I ferried countless children to and from Overton Primary School/Lordsfield Swimming Club and Testbourne. As a Liberal Democrat I have always supported localism so in 1994 I joined Overton Parish Council where I served for over two decades. During this time, with two years as Chair, I gained a wide range of experience from purchase of a community hall and helping to steer a new Neighbourhood Plan (NP) to repeatedly reporting potholes and clearing up after occasional bouts of vandalism. Today I am once again part of a small team steering Overton's NP Review and look forward to learning Overton residents' views and collaborating with the Whitchurch team.

Having previously worked in the pharmaceutical industry I am now the director of a small housing association. and Chair of Overton Business Association. With qualifications  in environmental management I am keen to work for greater energy efficiency and the introduction of new low carbon heating technologies in the home. We all need to keep warm but not at any price.

I am asking for your support to be a strong, independent voice for Winklebury and Manydown.

**My Focus**
- I'm here for our community first and always. My record of action speaks of my commitment to you.
- We need a local GP surgery, which will provide the services and facilities that we need.
- The needs of local residents must be at the heart of the Winklebury Regeneration Project.
- Housing associations must focus first and foremost on the needs of their tenants.
- The facilities on Manydown must benefit all of the residents in our ward.
- We must work with the Manydown Development team to minimise disruption to local residents due to construction work.

**My local experience**
I have lived in the ward since 2005. I have a background in nursing and management consultancy. I help to run The Barn Nursery pre-school in Wootton St Lawrence.

I am a former Borough Councillor for Winklebury and Manydown; Governor of Winklebury Federation Schools and former Governor of Ashwood Academy; Governor of Hampshire Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust; member of the Hampshire NHS Community Engagement Group; member of the Patient Participation Group of Watership Down Health; former member of the Winklebury Legends Committee; and member of the Parochial Church Council for Oakley with Wootton. I ran the annual Basingstoke Foodbank Christmas Hamper Project for 7 years.

As a Borough Councillor, I worked hard to support residents with issues and concerns, and spoke frequently in meetings at the council, representing local opinion. I also established and led the Winklebury Regeneration Review Panel, which campaigned on behalf of Winklebury residents for changes to Vivid's plans.

I believe strongly in working closely with all borough councillors, regardless of their political affiliation, in order to achieve the best for our ward and borough. Please vote for me to ensure your voice is heard and acted on in the Borough Council.  Thank you for your support.
I am committed to ensuring that the three tiers of Government work together for the re-generation of Winklebury for the benefit of all the residents
